Nyishi: Mvwg anne Mwam yolu ruqpa hoguv no lamta baakin wma den, alo otuv mwg lwchi atuw wto hel benum

English: The young crab responds,
Nyishi: Anyio bo tac mwe bwr pa,

English: “I would like to walk forward mom,
Nyishi: "ngo alov otuv wtesh mwl wden ann,

English: but I do not know how to”
Nyishi: Hwbemwbde, ngo cingma hoguv wgng maden,

English: Hearing this,
his mom gets up to show him how,
Nyishi: Mwg ann doore goruv layil katum dev nyinum hoguv wden hel,

English: but even she is unable to bend her knees forward
Nyishi: Hwbwbde, Ann mwe kam wtu yav lwbw nge lutun nyoma num

English: She realizes that she was being unfair,
Nyishi: Mwwe mwdar paku mw bwhah jipaq hel

English: apologizes sheepishly,
Nyishi: Ngam Apaq tukum hel wch yoma benum,

English: Moral:Don’t condemn someone for not doing something that you yourself are unable to do
Nyishi: Taguv se Agum:Nog nying manam mam asaq kan nyinang ma hel bwng mwav ka

English: and sits back in the sand
Nyishi: Hoge mvw sugur ho dokur kunam

English: The Dog At The Well
A dog and her pups lived on a farm,
Nyishi: Iky la mwg kwchu ko tulu nge boolu gol dodum,

English: where there was a well
Nyishi: Hog boolu ho sokam mwkam dodum

English: The mother dog told the pups,
Nyishi: Ik ann he ko ngam benum,

English: do not go near the well or play around it
Nyishi: aloq sokam dokuv la sokam bayum ho somav hel

English: One of the pups wondered why they shouldn’t go to the well and decided to explore it
Nyishi: Kwchu akine mwpa hoguv ngulam sokam agum wum maden,hoge mw kaagaqduv mwnum

English: He went to the well
Climbed up the wall and peeked inside

English: In there,
he saw his reflection and thought it was another dog
Nyishi: Mwg lvlaq kam katel mwpa si kwchu akda dopan hel

English: The pup saw that the other dog in the well (his reflection) was doing whatever he was doing,

English: and got angry for imitating him
Nyishi: Hoge mwe hang lengnum mwam nyi-nyima den mwlayil

English: He decided to fight with the dog and jumped into the well,
Nyishi: Mvwe mibwq kunam hog kwchu dooram mvmin sudkuv hel, hoge mvwe sokam urw poglq kunam

English: only to find no dog there
Nyishi: Hog sokam urw ho kwchu dwk manum

English: He barked and barked and swam until the farmer came and rescued him
Nyishi: Mvwe pwdw pwdw del donum hog mood gagub hedlo rongo mvnbo nyi hamadedev mvwe halayil Kwchu ngam nalin num

English: The pup had learned his lesson
Nyishi: Hog kwchu doore mvw pagaq kam cengpa kun

English: Moral:Always listen to what the elders say  Question them, but do not defy them
Nyishi: Taguv sa Agum:Lwxiam axag benammum tato Toxto hwbmwbde

English: The Man And The Cat
One day,
a man was walking by a road when he heard a cat meowing from the bushes nearby
Nyishi: Nyega doorg Lamta ho wl dodum ho mvwe tapapa nwmw bwyum hoge haas kabnam mam

English: The cat was stuck and needed help getting out
Nyishi: Haas doore coptu tel mwam nalin dev nyinum

English: When the man reached out,
the cat got scared and scratched the man
Nyishi: Haas doore boos ne ngega dooram hanum

English: The man screamed in pain but didn’t back down
Nyishi: Nyega doore sogog num wch hoge hwbwbde mvwe ukur manum

English: He tried again and again,
even as the cat continued to scratch his hands
Nyishi: Mvwe herav haas dooram napoq dev nyidul donum, mwam alaq ho hajaq kukam

English: Another passerby saw this and said,
Nyishi: Nyi ak da lamta ho wn bwnge kapatel benum,

English: “Just let it be!
The cat will find a way to come out later”
Nyishi: Haas mwe atte lendub lamta kapa sutayin kokwngam",

English: The man didn’t pay heed but tried until he helped the cat
Nyishi: Ngega doore hog benam mam teme-kalw tel haas dooram nalindev nyidu num, hoge mwe haas doora nalin kunam

English: Once he let the cat free,
Nyishi: Hedlo mwe haas dooram wam tukul

English: he told the other man,
Nyishi: Mwe lamta hog wn bo dooram bire num,

English: “The cat is an animal,
and its instincts make him scratch and attack
Nyishi: Hogav Bwlg mwwnam mw haduv la mwdev

English: I am a human and my instincts make me compassionate and kind”
Nyishi: Ngo nyi nge hogav ngog mwwnam mw anya mwwpadev la aldev hel"

English: Moral:Treat everyone around you like you want to be treated  Adhere to your own rules or ethics, not theirs
Nyishi: Taguv sa Agum:Nyi mwlegam nyito nam hogub nyitesa mwwden Nag nyiwa hoge nyij-muj ngam loxiam doog to

English: Good Company, Bad Company
Nyishi: Alobo Dvpar ajin, Almanbo Dvpar ajin

English: Two parrots built a nest in a banyan tree
Nyishi: Beet pvta vnyig siraq svvn ho osup supnum

English: They lived with their two young ones,
Nyishi: Bulv bulqg ko anyio vnyi lagv donum,

English: which they took good care of
Nyishi: Bunyiq ko ngam anyia jab sonum

English: The mother and the father parrot went out to gather food in the morning and came back home by evening
Nyishi: Ann la aab beet pvta bunyie soram nyirov dvnam nokum tav vpel ho oyum mam hadkun

English: One day,
when their parents were away,
Nyishi: Hedlo bunyiq ann aab bw adov vyro yuk ho,

English: the young parrots were taken by a cruel hunter
Nyishi: Anyio bo beet pvta ngam almonbo nyoro gaqkybo nge naal vwg kunam

English: One of the birds managed to escape and flew away from the hunter
Nyishi: Akine almanbo nyoro gaqkybo hoge kelen kuliye ado jab jwrl epaq kunam

English: He ended up at a hermitage and grew up listening to kind words and compassion
Nyishi: Ho mvwe vp cwkwb swwk nyook ho jwrcv kunam, hoge mvwe albo agumum la anya mwwpanum mum cingcalal swcha kunam

English: The hunter put the other parrot in a cage,
Nyishi: Ho nyoro gaqkybo nyi doore aken pvta ngum pwtwr ho tump kunam,

English: and soon it learned a few words and phrases
Nyishi: Ho vwyu madab mvwe anyio bo agum mum ceenalq kunam

English: The hunter and his family were crude and didn’t care much about kind words
Nyishi: Nyoro gaqkybo hoge mvw imiq nyie almonum hoge bule albo agumum anyia bevmanum

English: One day,
a passerby was resting outside the hunter’s hut
Nyishi: Nyi awk lamta hog wgnvbowe hog nyoro gaqkybo nam bayium ho donu donum

English: Sensing someone outside,
the parrot said,
why are you here? Fool! Leave!
Nyishi: No hogub so dopan? Paacha bo! Wtew!

English: I’ll cut your throat”
the traveler went away,
Nyishi: Mvw lamta hog wgalbonge ado wwknum,

English: and on his journey,
he reached the hermitage where the other parrot was
Nyishi: Mvw vp cwkwb swwk nyook ho wch kunam hoglo asaq beet pvta nge dwnyianum

English: The parrot at the hermitage spoke,
Nyishi: Ho vp cwkwb swwk nyook hog beet pvta nge benum,

English: “Welcome traveler
You are free to stay here as long as you want”
Nyishi: No swrso jav so doto hedgo no donw den"

English: Surprised,
the traveler told the parrot that he encountered a similar parrot elsewhere and it was very cruel
Nyishi: Ho wgagbo doore beet pvta doram benum, Mvw nog hev nyinbo pvta ak da kapanum hwbedwe mvwe alma num

English: How is that you are so kind?”
The parrot replied,
“That must be my brother
I live with the sages,
Nyishi: Ngo vcjaqb cengnbo nyi lagav dobaden,

English: and my brother lives with hunters
Nyishi: Ngag vbw hw nyoro gaqkybo nyi lagav doba den

English: I learned the sage’s language,
Nyishi: Ngo cengnbo nyi agum mem cengpa,

English: and my brother learned the hunter’s language
Nyishi: Ho ngog vbw he nyoro gaqganbo nyi agum mum cengpa

English: The company we keep decides who we will be”
Nyishi: Ngul dvpar nyw bibwq den ngule hoguv swtan hel"

English: Moral:Keep good company if you want to be a good person
Nyishi: Tagav sa Agum: Albo dvpar aap tow, no albo nyi akuv swnew denw lokam

English: An Old Man Lived in the Village
An old man lived in the village
Nyishi: Nyikam nyi ak nampam ho donum

English: He was one of the most unfortunate people in the world
Nyishi: Ho nyikam doore mwle ngam nunu yama bo nyi akuv nyinum geda soq

English: The whole village was tired of him
Nyishi: Mwam nampam mwle nyi nge wsuv ayav kunam

English: he was always gloomy,
Nyishi: Mwe lwxiam kan dvb kwda donum

English: he constantly complained and was always in a bad mood
Nyishi: Mwe lwxiam sordu twnum hoge alma donum

English: The longer he lived,
the more bile he was becoming and the more poisonous were his words
Nyishi: Hwdkalam nyikam doore gwnam mwka cumdwq kunam

English: People avoided him,
because his misfortune became contagious
Nyishi: Mwg oburnam mew mwlegam nyudku hel hoggab

English: It was even unnatural and insulting to be happy next to him
Nyishi: Hog luw he jvqtw maanam hoge nyinyi-nyiyanam hev nyinum mwg kwsin ho hvmp namw

English: He created the feeling of unhappiness in others
Nyishi: Hog nyikam doore mwg kwsin ho donbo mwlegam hvmp madev mwden

English: But one day,
when he turned eighty years old,
Nyishi: Hedlo mwe cam piin vnyi dwk ho,

English: an incredible thing happened
Instantly everyone started hearing the rumour:
Nyishi: Sija da nyi tulu nge pui nge yunam

English: “An Old Man is happy today,
he doesn’t complain about anything, smiles,
Nyishi: Mwe hoggokam sortum kuma sol, nyirs dodo,

English: and even his face is freshened up ”
Nyishi: Hoge mwg nyiume kam dwriq duku "

English: The whole village gathered together
The old man was asked:
Villager: What happened to you?
“Nothing special
Eighty years I’ve been chasing happiness,
Nyishi: Cam piin go ngo hvmp gav ruqga num

English: and it was useless
And then I decided to live without happiness and just enjoy life
Nyishi: Hoge ngo hwwbwq pakun hvmp doma jaqkukam ngog swnam sam alev swn kuv

English: That’s why I’m happy now ”
An Old Man
Moral:Don’t chase happiness  Enjoy your life
Nyishi: Tagab sa Agum:Hvmp gam ruqgam mav  Nog swnam alev swto

English: Having A Best Friend
A story tells that two friends were walking through the desert
Nyishi: So gon si Ajin vnyi go diixa-rara ho wldonam

English: During some point of the journey they had an argument,
Nyishi: wdudod ho bwng pwkcco haqyaq minsnum,

English: and one friend slapped the other one in the face
Nyishi: Ho ajin akine akin nyuqm ngam pitaqnum

English: The one who got slapped was hurt,
but without saying anything,
wrote in the sand
“Today my best friend slapped me in the face ”
Nyishi: "Sol ngog albo ajin ne ngam nyuqm so pitaqpa "

English: They kept on walking until they found an oasis,
Nyishi: Bwgne wdul donum hedlo pobu dokum wgch ma dedev

English: they aggred to take bath
The one who had been slapped got stuck in the mire and started drowning,
Nyishi: Hog pitaqnam doore suxu ho xuq tel wrav yoql donum,

English: but the friend saved him
Nyishi: Hwbmwbde mwg ajin doore ywwlin num

English: After he recovered from the near drowning,
he wrote on a stone
“Today my best friend saved my life ”
Nyishi: "Sol ngog albo ajin ne ngag swg ngam ywwlin jipa "

English: The friend who had slapped and saved his best friend asked him
Nyishi: Ho ajin pitaq hoge ywwlin bo doore akiin nam toxw num

English: After I hurt you,
you wrote in the sand and now,
Nyishi: No xuqxy ho xeqk pel hoge sega,

English: you write on a stone, why?”
Nyishi: No wlw ho xeqk pa, hoguv?"

English: The other friend replied
“When someone hurts us we should write it down in sand where winds of forgiveness can erase it away
Nyishi: "Nam hwdlo nyi nge almaqav nying lokam xuqxy ho xeqkto hoglo apaqjinam dooi nge ado tinxaq dev

English: when someone does something good for us,
Nyishi: Hwdlo nyi nge ngulam alev nying lokam ,

English: we must engrave it in stone where no wind can ever erase it ”
Nyishi: Ngul ham wlw ho xeqto hwdlo kam dooi ye yiiha nyoma dev

English: Moral:Don’t value the things you have in your life  But value who you have in your life
Nyishi: Taguv sa agum: Nog dvrab dognam xima bw  hwbnawbde, nog hiyv nyi nge nog lwguv doden ham xito

English: The Greedy Lion
It was an incredibly hot day,
Nyishi: Hogho mwwnyianyima num og al ho,

English: and a lion was feeling very hungry
Nyishi: Hoge sonyi nyot doore mwwdwpa vcuv kanojagpa hel

English: He came out of his den and searched here and there
Nyishi: Mwe mwg wng hoge lenlayil mmeegagnum tolo-bolo

English: He could find only a small hare
Nyishi: Mwe kaapanum buut kubu anyio ko ak

English: He caught the hare with some hesitation
Nyishi: Mwe nwtw pa anyio bo sudum dooram hanyilayil

English: “This hare can’t fill my stomach”
Nyishi: "Sog buut kubu si ngog kepo gav jwt ram hel"

English: thought the lion
As the lion was about to kill the hare,
Nyishi: Sonyi doore buut kubu dooram mengta dwkho,

English: a deer ran that way
The lion became greedy
He thought
“Instead of eating this small hare,
Nyishi: "Anyiobo buut kubu sam deta mwab kwlib,

English: let me eat the big deer ”
Nyishi: Sudum doort bo alam ngo dedev mwwpa

English: He let the hare go and went behind the deer
Nyishi: Mwwe kubu buut dooram wmtukul, hoge sudum doort bo kokw wngpa

English: But the deer had vanished into the forest
Nyishi: Hwbmwbde sudum doore nyooro ho nyileq kunam,

English: The lion now felt sorry for letting the hare off
Nyishi: Sonyi doore kubu but dooram wwam nam mwwkurkunam

English: Moral:A bird in hand is worth two in the bush
Nyishi: Taguv sa Agum:Alaq so pvta nge nwmwv gav xinam vnyi xinamw

English: Two Friends & The Bear
Vijay and Raju were friends
On a holiday they went walking into a forest,
Nyishi: Doonu aal ho bwnyie mooro ho wl donum,

English: enjoying the beauty of nature
Nyishi: Hemp pwjo layil Anyia bo nwsin nwmw laguv

English: Suddenly they saw a bear coming at them
Nyishi: Cingmapaq bwnyie sutum ak hadu namam kapapa

English: They became frightened
who knew all about climbing trees,
ran up to a tree and climbed up quickly
Nyishi: Svn aww hwngcha pa nyibav ja

English: He didn’t think of Vijay
Vijay had no idea how to climb the tree
Nyishi: Vijay mwwe cingmanum svn aww caww dev

English: Vijay thought for a second
He’d heard animals don’t prefer dead bodies,
Nyishi: Mwwe tapa svdin nge nyi sinbo ga ngam dema den hel,

English: so he fell to the ground and held his breath
Nyishi: Hoge mwwe nyikw ho karp num hoge mww sak mav donum

English: The bear sniffed him and thought he was dead
Nyishi: Hog sutum doore mwwm suka pekul mwwpa sipak hel

English: So, it went on its way
Raju asked Vijay
“What did the bear whisper into your ears?”
Nyishi: "Hogge nake sutume nam nyrw ho bespa hel?"

English: Vijay replied,
“The bear asked me to keep away from friends like you”
Nyishi: "Sutume begume nog hek ajin hoge ado to hel"

English: and went on his way
Moral:A friend in need is a friend indeed
Nyishi: Taguv sa Agum:Ajin nwwdo ajinam jvqtwjaqb

English: The Lion & The Poor Slave
Nyishi: Sonyi Nyoot La Jwwgw bo Nyira

English: A slave,
ill-treated by his master,
Nyishi: Nyinyi nyiya kotel mwg atv hoge

English: runs away to the forest
There he comes across a lion in pain because of a thorn in his paw
Nyishi: Ho mwwe Sonyi doorog kapanum vch dev, hog lvpia ho taa nge dugav tel donum

English: The slave bravely goes forward and removes the thorn gently
Nyishi: Nyira doore haakamjav otuv vlayil hog sonyi lvpia hog taa ngam mvpaq jinam

English: The lion without hurting him goes away
Nyishi: Sonyi doore nyira akam hoggokam nyimav ado vle kunam

English: Some days later,
the slave’s master comes hunting to the forest and catches many animals and cages them
Nyishi: Nyira door attw mooro gaqtav moorow vnum, vchev svdin nge notu layil pwtwr ho vmapnum

English: The slave is spotted by the masters’ men who catch him and bring him to the cruel master
Nyishi: Nyira dooram mwwg attwg nyinge kapalayil nwtu ne almanbo attw door dokum bwch jiknum

English: The master asks for the slave to be thrown into the lion’s cage
Nyishi: Att ake paan nyira tulu ngam paat gv ung ho hvrlungto hvl benum.

English: The slave is awaiting his death in the cage when he realizes that it is the same lion that he had helped
Nyishi: Nyira doore mwwg sigam kayayal pwtwr ho dodho mwe mwdwr pa hog sonyi si mwwg ywwlin nam hepahel

English: The slave rescued the lion and all other caged animals
Nyishi: Nyira mwwe sonyi dooram ywwlin layil hoge pwtwr hog donbo mwwleg svdin ngam kam ywwlin kunam

English: Moral :One should help others in need, we get the rewards of our helpful acts in return
Nyishi: Taguv Sa Agum: Nyinge nyi asaq kam svka dev nyiden, ngule ywwlinam nyijo aminam nam herv kapatayin

English: The Fox & The Grapes
One afternoon a fox was walking through the forest and spotted a bunch of grapes hanging from over a lofty branch
Nyishi: Al doony tab tuk ho siyal mwwe moorow ho wkarnum, ho mwwe roo aaxh ngam haqb hoge bwyio dev kapanum

English: “Just the thing to quench my thirst,” he thought
Taking a few steps back,
he fox jumped and just missed the hanging grapes
Nyishi: Siyal mwwe pwgca pa hevgekam, mwwe pogxa pakun

English: Again the fox took a few paces back and tried to reach them but still failed
Nyishi: Logda Siyal mwwe anyio go wkurlayil roo ngam wch dev nyipa herav pogxapa

English: Finally,
giving up,
the fox turned up his nose and said,
Nyishi: Siyal mwge nyipum mam tuxi layil benum

English: They’re probably sour anyway,”
Nyishi: Toloq roo aaxy to kusuq pade hel,"

English: and proceeded to walk away
Nyishi: Hoge wruv pakun ado wdd kuv

English: Moral:It’s easy to despise what you can’t have
Nyishi: Taguv Sa Agum: HNog nanying manum

English: Be Good To Your Enemies
Long ago,
there lived a little boy named Sammy
Nyishi: Nyiga ko anyio bo ak donum ho emine Sammy hel

English: He was a good boy  He was good in his studies,
Nyishi: Mwwe all jinbo nyiga gov nyinum  Mwwg poorynge alpa,

English: obedient to his parents,
more intelligent than many other boys in his class and kind to everyone
Nyishi: Vchv cenglaq yanum asaq nyega ko hoge, herv mwlengam anyia num

English: Grown-ups as well as those junior to Sammy loved him very much
Nyishi: Akam tel hoge mwwam anyio yan bo tulu nge mwwam vchev aby num

English: But that aroused jealousy in many other boys who longed to be as loved as Sammy
Nyishi: Hwbmwbde kaaneq bo ngam nalq donum, heyie Sammy hev kelam aby nwniden

English: Now there was another boy named Timmy who studied in the same class as Sammy
Nyishi: Hoge, Nyega ko asaq ak kam donum hog emine Timmy hel, mwwe Sammy lakuv poory riyin num

English: Unlike Sammy,
he was not good at studies and always liked to play during school hours
Nyishi: Mwwe poory ho kam almanum, hoge School poorydw ngam sokar num

English: He misbehaved with his parents,
Nyishi: Mwwe ann abb ngam kam nyixiaq num

English: bullied his classmates and even ill-treated Sammy
Nyishi: Mwwg poory reyin ajin nam nying-nyiya num hoge Sammy nwn kam almanum

English: He always tried to put Sammy down and belittled him before other kids in the class
Nyishi: Lwxiam mwwe Sammy nen ako katum dev hoge almadev katum dev mwlege ko asaq lo

English: But no matter what he did,
Nyishi: Hwbmwbde, mwwe hoguv kan nyijaq kukam,

English: Sammy's grades kept getting better and better
Nyishi: Sammy ge poory nge al yayia dodum

English: Whether in studies or in sports or from his classmates,
Nyishi: poory ho, hoge soka mwngus nam jaqkam mwwg ajin tulu hoge,

English: Sammy kept getting accolades from everywhere
Nyishi: Sammy mwwlege hoge paqkum namguv nyinum

English: On his eighth birthday,
Nyishi: Mwwg piin ko bed al ho,

English: Sammy got a nice pen as a gift from his parents
Nyishi: Sammy ann aabe mwwam xeqn nan ak lvqc cenum

English: He brought it to school so that he could use it to take down the notes of the lectures that the teachers gave in class
Nyishi: Ham mwwe school ho baqwa layil xeqn duv hoge, mwwg poory reg bo tulu betum nam ngam

English: This was a very beautiful pen and it could help one write very fast
Nyishi: Hog xeqn nan he vchv anyia num hoge xeqn dev aljanum

English: When Timmy saw it,
he was very jealous of Sammy
Nyishi: Mwwe Sammy vchev kaaneq ja nume

English: He asked Sammy,
Hey, where did you get that? Did you buy it?
Nyishi: "No hem hoglo nge napan? Nog rwnum mwye?"

English: My parents gave it as a birthday gift to me
Nyishi: "Ngog ann aabe ngam ko bwd ho lvc cenamme "

English: replied Sammy
Timmy was overwhelmed with anger and jealousy
Nyishi: Timmy mwwe kaaneq la hang lengmnamne mviya kunam

English: The bad boy that he was,
he rarely got any present from his parents
Nyishi: Mwwg ann aabe log logav mwwam lvc jenum

English: He decided to steal Sammy's pen
Nyishi: Mwwe Sammy Xaqn nen ham doocho dev mwwnum

English: During recess,
when everyone had gone out from the class,
Nyishi: Hwdlo mwwle nge poory tav uyum yekun,

English: Timmy opened Sammy's bag and took out his pen
Nyishi: Tmmy mwwe Sammy cuk ngam mwwko layil hog xeqn nen ngam napakun

English: Then he hid it inside his bag and went out to have his tiffin
Nyishi: hoge mwwe mwwg cuk wrre ho kvs tel hoge mwwe dvnam detav wle num

English: When Sammy came back and could not find his pen,
Nyishi: Hwdlo Sammy hakur duk ho, mwwg Xeqn nenum kaapa kuma nume,

English: he informed his class teacher about it
Nyishi: Mwwe mwwg poory rwg bo ngam betum num

English: There was a hunt for the missing pen and the class teacher ordered the class monitor to search the bag of every children inside the class
Nyishi: Hog xeqn nenam mwwle nge meegaqnum, hoge poory reg bo nge bwwleg koochum bo ngam bepa mwwlg cuk urw ngam kam kato hel

English: The missing pen was soon found out of Timmy's bag and the furious teacher asked the errant boy,
Nyishi: Ho nyin bo xeqn nenam Timmy nge cuk hoge kapakunam, hoge hang lengtel mwwam tox num,

English: Now Timmy, what do you have to say about it?
Timmy was in tears  He had nothing to say
Nyishi: Timmy mwwe nyiqs lenpa  Mwwe hoggokam berq kumwb

English: When Sammy saw Timmy cry,
Nyishi: Hwdlo Sammy mwwe Timmy kabnum mam kapatel,

English: he took pity on the boy
Nyishi: Mwwe anyia mwwpa num hog nyega ko ham

English: The kind boy that he was,
he had no ill-feeling against his classmate
Nyishi: Mwwe almav mwwma num mwwg poory rin num

English: He requested his class teacher not to take any action against Timmy,
Nyishi: Mwwe poory req bo ham humpa Timmy nen jwwmam hel

English: now that his stolen pen was found
Nyishi: Seja mwwg nyin bo xeqn nen nanam kapapakun

English: This opened Timmy's eyes
Nyishi: Sog nyinam me Timmy vyin kam mwwko jipkun

English: He could now see what a good boy Sammy was
Nyishi: Sija mwwe kapku Sammy hwdgo albo nyega ko go hel
